purpose: Route greenfield product requests through startup pressure testing and a unified spec-pipeline stage without skipping prerequisites.
selection_order: If the real question is whether the thesis survives, route to startup-pressure-test first.
If the product direction is already validated and the work is moving toward a spec (blueprint → requirements → system design), route to spec-pipeline.
If the request would skip a prerequisite stage, route to the prerequisite first — within the pipeline if appropriate, or to startup-pressure-test if viability hasn't been assessed.

children.name: startup-pressure-test
children.summary: Pressure-tests a startup thesis, launch plan, GTM path, and early unit economics before blueprinting goes too far.
route_when: The real question is startup viability, GTM realism, CAC/churn/runway, or whether the business survives market contact.
The idea is still unproven enough that a commercial teardown should happen before strategy or PRD work.
avoid_when: The direction is already validated and the work is strategy / blueprint creation.
The user already asked for spec pipeline work downstream of viability assessment.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing if the runtime cannot discover bundled children automatically.

children.name: spec-pipeline
children.summary: Runs the full product spec pipeline: strategic blueprint → requirements → system design, producing .agents-stack/reference/design.md, requirements.md, and architecture/implementation.md.
route_when: The product direction is validated and the work is creating the product spec (blueprint, requirements, or system design).
The user needs any combination of MVP strategy, PRD, user stories, acceptance criteria, schemas, API contracts, or infrastructure decisions.
avoid_when: The main need is startup viability teardown.
The output needed is purely implementation work that already has spec artifacts.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing if the runtime cannot discover bundled children automatically.
